precedents.title: Kesavananda Bharati v. State of Kerala (1973)
precedents.simplified_explanation: Supreme Court ne kaha ki Parliament Constitution badal sakti hai, lekin uske 'Basic Structure' ko touch nahi kar sakti. Yeh democracy ko bachane wala sabse bada case hai.
precedents.professional_analysis: Established the 'Basic Structure Doctrine'. Held that while Parliament has the power to amend the Constitution under Article 368, such power does not extend to altering its essential features.

precedents.title: Maneka Gandhi v. Union of India (1978)
precedents.simplified_explanation: Case ne clear kiya ki kisi ki freedom lene ke liye kanoon 'fair' aur 'just' hona chahiye. Sirf kanoon hona kaafi nahi hai.
precedents.professional_analysis: Expanded the scope of Article 21. Established that 'Procedure established by law' must be just, fair, and reasonable, incorporating the principle of Substantive Due Process.

precedents.title: Vishaka v. State of Rajasthan (1997)
precedents.simplified_explanation: Workplace par sexual harassment rokne ke liye pehli baar rules (Guidelines) diye gaye jo baad mein POSH Act bana.
precedents.professional_analysis: Formulated the 'Vishaka Guidelines' to address sexual harassment at the workplace in the absence of enacted legislation, drawing from CEDAW (International Law).

precedents.title: Navtej Singh Johar v. Union of India (2018)
precedents.simplified_explanation: Supreme Court ne Section 377 ko khatam kiya aur kaha ki LGBTQ community ke rights bhi Fundamental Rights hain.
precedents.professional_analysis: Decriminalized consensual homosexual acts between adults by reading down Section 377 of the IPC, citing violations of Articles 14, 15, 19, and 21.

precedents.title: Justice K.S. Puttaswamy v. Union of India (2017)
precedents.simplified_explanation: Court ne announce kiya ki Privacy har Bharatiya ka ek Fundamental Right hai jo Article 21 ke andar aata hai.
precedents.professional_analysis: Unanimously held that the Right to Privacy is a fundamental right protected under Part III of the Constitution, specifically under Article 21's Right to Life and Liberty.

bns_mappings.change: Section number changed from 302 to 101. Punishment remains Death or Life Imprisonment.
bns_mappings.simplified: Ab Murder ki dhara 101 hogi. Saza umrakaid ya maut hi rahegi.

bns_mappings.change: Section 307 is now Section 109 in BNS.
bns_mappings.simplified: Maut ki koshish (Attempt to Murder) ab Section 109 ke andar aayegi.

bns_mappings.change: Detailed categorization. False promise to marry now explicitly covered.
bns_mappings.simplified: Rape ke kanoon ab Section 63-69 mein hain. Shaadi ka jhutha wada karke relation banana ab clear crime hai.
Chori (Theft) ki nayi dhara Section 303 hai.

bns_mappings.change: Cheating moves from famously known 420 to 318.
Word 'Sedition' removed. Focus shifted to acts endangering sovereignty/integrity of India.
bns_mappings.simplified: Sedition शब्द hata diya gaya hai. Ab 'Bharat ki ekta aur akhandata ko khatra' pahunchana crime hai Section 152 mein.

glossary.meaning: The objective legal reason for a court's decision.
A judge's expression of opinion uttered in court or in a written judgement, but not essential to the decision.
Guilty mind; the intention or knowledge of wrongdoing.
The physical act of committing a crime.
